atomic.c(3) Library Functions Manual atomic.c(3)

NAME

atomic.c - Debug formatting and state tracking for atomic operations.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


void debug_atomic_init (void)
void debug_atomic_shutdown (void)
bool debug_atomic_is_initialized (void)
int debug_atomic_format_timing (const atomic_t *atomic, char *buffer, size_t size)
Format timing info for an atomic and write to buffer. int debug_atomic_ptr_format_timing (const atomic_ptr_t *atomic, char *buffer, size_t size)
Format timing info for an atomic_ptr_t and write to buffer. void debug_atomic_print_state (void)

Detailed Description

Debug formatting and state tracking for atomic operations.

Date

March 2026

Implements debug-specific functions: initialization/shutdown, and state formatting for display via --sync-state.

Hook implementations (atomic_on_load, atomic_on_store, etc.) are now in lib/atomic.c alongside the wrapper functions they support.

Definition in file debug/atomic.c.

Function Documentation

int debug_atomic_format_timing (const atomic_t * atomic, char * buffer, size_t size)

Format timing info for an atomic and write to buffer.

Parameters

atomic Pointer to atomic_t
buffer Output buffer
size Buffer size

Returns

Number of bytes written to buffer

Definition at line 51 of file debug/atomic.c.

51                                                                                   {
52     if (!atomic || !buffer || size == 0) return 0;
53 
54     // If never accessed, return empty (so caller can skip output)
55     if (atomic->last_load_time_ns == 0 && atomic->last_store_time_ns == 0) {
56         return 0;
57     }
58 
59     int offset = 0;
60     uint64_t now_ns = time_get_ns();
61 
62     char load_str[64] = "";
63     char store_str[64] = "";
64     char count_str[128] = "";
65 
66     if (atomic->last_load_time_ns > 0 && atomic->last_load_time_ns <= now_ns) {
67         char elapsed_str[64];
68         time_pretty(now_ns - atomic->last_load_time_ns, -1, elapsed_str, sizeof(elapsed_str));
69         snprintf(load_str, sizeof(load_str), "load=%s", elapsed_str);
70     }
71 
72     if (atomic->last_store_time_ns > 0 && atomic->last_store_time_ns <= now_ns) {
73         char elapsed_str[64];
74         time_pretty(now_ns - atomic->last_store_time_ns, -1, elapsed_str, sizeof(elapsed_str));
75         snprintf(store_str, sizeof(store_str), "store=%s", elapsed_str);
76     }
77 
78     if (atomic->load_count > 0 || atomic->store_count > 0 || atomic->cas_count > 0 || atomic->fetch_count > 0) {
79         snprintf(count_str, sizeof(count_str), "[ops: load=%lu store=%lu cas=%lu/%lu fetch=%lu]",
80                  atomic->load_count, atomic->store_count, atomic->cas_success_count, atomic->cas_count, atomic->fetch_count);
81     }
82 
83     offset += snprintf(buffer + offset, size - offset, "%s %s %s", load_str, store_str, count_str);
84     return offset;
85 }




References time_get_ns(), and time_pretty().

  debug_atomic_init (void )

28                              {
29     g_atomic_debug_initialized = true;
30 }

38 }

    atomic_on_load(), atomic_on_store(),
    atomic_ptr_on_cas(), atomic_ptr_on_exchange(),
    atomic_ptr_on_load(), and atomic_ptr_on_store().






void
  debug_atomic_print_state (void )


Definition at line 130 of file debug/atomic.c.


130                                     {
131     if (!g_atomic_debug_initialized) return;
132 
133     // Iterate named registry for atomic entries
134     // Note: this is now kept for backward compatibility
135     // The preferred method is to use sync.c's atomic_iter_callback
136     // which integrates atomics into the synchronized output buffer
137 }










int
  debug_atomic_ptr_format_timing (const atomic_ptr_t * atomic, char * buffer,
  size_t size)


Format timing info for an atomic_ptr_t and write to buffer.


Parameters


atomic Pointer to atomic_ptr_t


buffer Output buffer


size Buffer size


Returns


Number of bytes written to buffer


Definition at line 94 of file debug/atomic.c.


94                                                                                           {
95     if (!atomic || !buffer || size == 0) return 0;
96 
97     // If never accessed, return empty (so caller can skip output)
98     if (atomic->last_load_time_ns == 0 && atomic->last_store_time_ns == 0) {
99         return 0;
100     }
101 
102     int offset = 0;
103     uint64_t now_ns = time_get_ns();
104 
105     char load_str[64] = "";
106     char store_str[64] = "";
107     char count_str[128] = "";
108 
109     if (atomic->last_load_time_ns > 0 && atomic->last_load_time_ns <= now_ns) {
110         char elapsed_str[64];
111         time_pretty(now_ns - atomic->last_load_time_ns, -1, elapsed_str, sizeof(elapsed_str));
112         snprintf(load_str, sizeof(load_str), "load=%s", elapsed_str);
113     }
114 
115     if (atomic->last_store_time_ns > 0 && atomic->last_store_time_ns <= now_ns) {
116         char elapsed_str[64];
117         time_pretty(now_ns - atomic->last_store_time_ns, -1, elapsed_str, sizeof(elapsed_str));
118         snprintf(store_str, sizeof(store_str), "store=%s", elapsed_str);
119     }
120 
121     if (atomic->load_count > 0 || atomic->store_count > 0 || atomic->cas_count > 0 || atomic->exchange_count > 0) {
122         snprintf(count_str, sizeof(count_str), "[ops: load=%lu store=%lu cas=%lu/%lu exchange=%lu]",
123                  atomic->load_count, atomic->store_count, atomic->cas_success_count, atomic->cas_count, atomic->exchange_count);
124     }
125 
126     offset += snprintf(buffer + offset, size - offset, "%s %s %s", load_str, store_str, count_str);
127     return offset;
128 }
